mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline model, with a walk-forward Sharpe ratio below 0.5 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which drives positive equity returns over the next 20 trading days during economic recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns during recovery periods, with a t-statistic below 1.5 for the predictive coefficient.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation exceeding a 0.4% monthly threshold forces expectations of tighter monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 70% of high-inflation threshold crossings, invalidating the valuation compression channel.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, supporting wage growth and consumer confidence, which yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal shows a negative or zero Rank IC over rolling 2-year windows, indicating no predictive joint effect.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.5% annualized confirms strong aggregate demand, which directly boosts corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 2.5% fails to predict positive 20-day returns, with out-of-sample directional accuracy below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production signal improving factory output and supply chain efficiency, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month changes show no predictive power for 5-day returns, with a p-value greater than 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.
